title: Crowd Dataset
subtitle: A large scale benchmark for crowd understanding, counting, localisation, and retrieval.
highlight: This starter is made for a single dataset website. Edit this JSON file, swap in your assets, and push to your GitHub Pages repository.
overview: Crowd Dataset is a template entry standing in for your real dataset. Replace this paragraph with a concise abstract that explains what the dataset contains, why it matters, and how it should be used. This section is deliberately styled like a research project page rather than a product landing page.

examples.title: Sparse scene
examples.description: A lower density scene with clear perspective variation.

examples.title: Medium density scene
examples.description: Representative sample with partial occlusions and scale changes.

examples.title: High density scene
examples.description: A highly congested scene with dense annotations.

resources.title: Images and annotations
resources.description: Host a release archive, Hugging Face dataset, or cloud bucket here.

resources.title: Format and evaluation
resources.description: Add details about annotation format, directory structure, and evaluation scripts.

resources.title: Reference code
resources.description: Point to a baseline implementation, training code, or challenge starter kit.

faq.question: How do I add my own examples?
faq.answer: Put your images in assets/examples and update the examples array in data/site.json.

faq.question: Do I need React, Node, or Jekyll?
faq.answer: No. This starter is plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, so it works well with GitHub Pages.

faq.question: Can I add a leaderboard?
faq.answer: Yes. For a static table, edit the benchmark array. For a live leaderboard, fetch a JSON file from your repository or another endpoint.

footerNote: Built as a static site for GitHub Pages
